challenges.title: Implement BEM on a Component
challenges.description: Take the given unstyled HTML and apply BEM naming to create a well-architected product card component. Requirements: (1) use proper BEM block/element/modifier naming, (2) create at least one modifier variant (e.g., featured, compact), (3) write CSS with flat selectors (no nesting beyond BEM structure), (4) include: product image, title, description, price, rating stars, add-to-cart button.
hints: The block is the outer container: .product-card
Use BEM even for nested blocks (e.g., a button inside the card is still .btn, not .product-card__btn)
challenges.starterCode:
Wireless Headphones

★★★★☆

Premium wireless headphones with noise cancellation.

$79.99
challenges.solution: /* Block */ .product-card { background: #fff; border-radius: 12px; overflow: hidden; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } /* Elements */ .product-card__image { width: 100%; height: 200px; object-fit: cover; } .product-card__body { padding: 16px; display: grid; gap: 8px; } .product-card__title { font-size: 1.25rem; font-weight: 700; } .product-card__rating { color: #f5b342; font-size: 0.9rem; } .product-card__desc { color: #666; font-size: 0.9rem; } .product-card__price { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; color: #333; } .product-card__btn { background: #667eea; color: white; border: none; padding: 10px; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; font-weight: 700; } /* Modifiers */ .product-card--featured { border: 2px solid #667eea; } .product-card--sale .product-card__price::after { content: ' SALE!'; color: #e74c3c; font-size: 0.8rem; }

challenges.title: Design Token Migration
challenges.description: You have been given a stylesheet with hard-coded values everywhere. Refactor it to use CSS custom properties (design tokens). Requirements: (1) identify all hard-coded color, spacing, typography, and border-radius values, (2) define them as :root custom properties, (3) replace all hard-coded values with var() references, (4) create a dark theme variant using [data-theme='dark'], (5) ensure the theme switching works when the data-theme attribute changes, (6) document the token naming convention.
hints: For dark theme, only override the tokens that change
challenges.solution: :root { /* Colors */ --color-primary: #667eea; --color-surface: #ffffff; --color-text: #333333; --color-text-inverse: #ffffff; /* Spacing */ --spacing-md: 16px; --spacing-lg: 24px; --spacing-xl: 32px; /* Typography */ --font-sans: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; --text-heading: 1.5rem; /* Borders */ --radius-md: 8px; --radius-lg: 12px; --shadow-card: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); } [data-theme='dark'] { --color-primary: #89b4fa; --color-surface: #1e1e2e; --color-text: #cdd6f4; --shadow-card: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.4); } .card { background: var(--color-surface); color: var(--color-text); padding: var(--spacing-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-lg); box-shadow: var(--shadow-card); } .card h2 { font-size: var(--text-heading); margin-bottom: var(--spacing-md); } .btn { background: var(--color-primary); color: var(--color-text-inverse); padding: var(--spacing-md) var(--spacing-lg); border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; font-family: var(--font-sans); }

challenges.title: Architect a Full Component Library with Layers
challenges.description: Design and implement a small component library using ITCSS layers and BEM naming. Requirements: (1) Define @layer order: settings, generic, elements, objects, components, utilities, (2) Create at least 3 different components (button, card, modal), (3) Use BEM naming for all components, (4) Create 2 layout objects (grid and flex container), (5) Create utility classes for margin, padding, text alignment, (6) Use CSS custom properties for all design tokens, (7) Each component has at least 2 modifier variants, (8) The modal component should include an overlay with backdrop-filter, (9) Test that utilities override components (via layer order).
hints: Layer order matters: @layer settings, generic, elements, objects, components, utilities
Unlayered styles beat all layers — put utilities in a layer to keep them controllable
The modal overlay is a good use case for backdrop-filter
challenges.solution: The @layer structure above IS the solution. Key points: (1) Layers are declared in order, (2) Utilities come last so they override components, (3) .o- prefix for layouts, .c- prefix for components, .u- prefix for utilities, (4) Modal uses is-open state class (SMACSS pattern), (5) Custom properties centralize all design values.
